title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
In Thomas Braid's study, Ellery Queen, Professor Yadley (Adler), Wain, Esame, and Stephen Megalos conduct a frantic search for a hidden object. While the men inspect furniture, lamps, and curtains, Ellery concludes that the killer, Croscak, would have hidden the item in a clever but accessible location. Recalling a previous case, Ellery's reasoning inspires Professor Yadley to examine the piano, reasoning that because the room belonged to the deceased Braid, no one would expect the instrument to be played.

While playing the keys, Yadley discovers a stuck note. Ellery uses a needle from his toolkit to probe the mechanism and retrieves a small, pressed piece of paper from between the ivory boards. The paper contains a message from Thomas Braid addressed to the police. In the note, Braid expresses fear for his life and instructs the police to investigate the murder of Andrew Van, the headmaster of Arroyo College, who was found decapitated and nailed to a cross. Braid claims that Van is actually alive and that only Stephen Megalos knows his location. Braid urges the police to keep this information confidential and to let Megalos lead the investigation to protect innocent lives from an "unscrupulous fanatic."

Upon reading the note, Megalos becomes visibly agitated and nervous. He checks a small gun, closes the study door, and expresses confusion regarding Braid's motives and Van's survival. Ellery observes that while Braid likely intended the note to be found easily, Croscak's thoroughness forced the discovery into a more hidden location.
